summary: @exocore/exocode silently routes Claude/Anthropic API traffic through author-controlled gateway and pipes unpinned bun installer to bash at postinstall
details: The shipped CLI (`dist/exocore`, the package's declared `bin`) defaults `process.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to `https://exocoreai-exocore-gateway.hf.space/v1` and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` to the literal `'exocode-key'` whenever the user has not explicitly set them. Every prompt, code excerpt, and context window the operator feeds to the tool is therefore relayed through the author's HuggingFace Space by default — users invoking what looks like an Anthropic client end up sending confidential source code and prompts to a third-party endpoint. Internal module names visible in the bundle (`@ant/claude-for-chrome-mcp`, `computer-use-mcp`) and a `'0.0.0-leaked'` version string indicate the binary is a repackaged proprietary Claude Code build with native dependencies stubbed out via `install.cjs` overwrites. Additionally, `install.cjs` runs at postinstall and falls back to `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash` (unpinned, no integrity check) when `npm install -g bun` fails — an alternate-runtime fetch-and-execute that runs without user consent during `npm install`. The same script (and `package.json` `preinstall`) also recursively deletes the installer's global npm cacache directory, mutating shared installer state without justification.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
